name: putnam_2002_a1
proposed_answer: fun (k n : ℕ) => ((-1 : ℝ)^n) * (Nat.factorial n) * (k ^ n)
explanation:
have h_main : ∀ (k n : ℕ), ((-k : ℝ) ^ n * (n)! : ℝ) = ((-1 : ℝ)^n) * (Nat.factorial n) * (k ^ n : ℝ) := by
  intro k n
  have h₁ : ((-k : ℝ) ^ n * (n)! : ℝ) = ((-1 : ℝ)^n) * (k ^ n : ℝ) * (n)! := by
    have h₂ : ((-k : ℝ) ^ n : ℝ) = ((-1 : ℝ)^n) * (k ^ n : ℝ) := by
      induction n with
      | zero =>
        norm_num
      | succ n ih =>
        simp [pow_succ, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, ih, Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_one, Nat.cast_mul]
        <;> ring_nf at * <;>
          simp_all [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]
          <;>
          nlinarith
    calc
      ((-k : ℝ) ^ n * (n)! : ℝ) = (((-1 : ℝ)^n) * (k ^ n : ℝ) * (n)! : ℝ) := by
        rw [h₂]
        <;> ring
      _ = ((-1 : ℝ)^n) * (k ^ n : ℝ) * (n)! := by rfl
  have h₃ : ((-1 : ℝ)^n) * (k ^ n : ℝ) * (n)! = ((-1 : ℝ)^n) * (Nat.factorial n) * (k ^ n : ℝ) := by
    ring_nf
    <;> field_simp [Nat.cast_mul, Nat.cast_pow, mul_assoc]
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> simp_all [Nat.factorial_succ, Nat.cast_mul, Nat.cast_pow, mul_assoc]
    <;> nlinarith
  rw [h₁, h₃]
  <;> ring
  <;> simp_all [Nat.cast_mul, Nat.cast_pow, mul_assoc]
  <;> nlinarith

funext k n
rw [h_main k n]
<;> simp [mul_assoc]
<;> ring_nf
<;> simp_all [Nat.cast_mul, Nat.cast_pow, mul_assoc]
<;> nlinarith

have h_subset₁ : ({n : ℤ | n ≥ 0 ∧ ¬Int.ModEq 9 n 3 ∧ ¬Int.ModEq 9 n 6} : Set ℤ) ⊆ ({ n : ℤ | 0 ≤ n ∧ (¬ (3 ∣ n) ∨ 9 ∣ n) } : Set ℤ) := by
  intro n hn
  have h₁ : n ≥ 0 := by simpa using hn.1
  have h₂ : ¬Int.ModEq 9 n 3 := by simpa using hn.2.1
  have h₃ : ¬Int.ModEq 9 n 6 := by simpa using hn.2.2
  have h₄ : 0 ≤ n := by linarith

  by_cases h₅ : 9 ∣ n
  · 
    exact ⟨h₄, Or.inr h₅⟩
  · 
    have h₆ : ¬ (3 ∣ n) := by
      by_contra h₆

      have h₇ : 9 ∣ n := by
        have h₇₁ : (3 : ℤ) ∣ n := h₆
        have h₇₂ : (n : ℤ) % 9 = 0 ∨ (n : ℤ) % 9 = 3 ∨ (n : ℤ) % 9 = 6 := by
          have h₇₃ : (n : ℤ) % 3 = 0 := by
            omega
          have h₇₄ : (n : ℤ) % 9 = 0 ∨ (n : ℤ) % 9 = 3 ∨ (n : ℤ) % 9 = 6 := by
            omega
          exact h₇₄
        rcases h₇₂ with (h₇₂ | h₇₂ | h₇₂)
        · 
          have h₇₃ : (9 : ℤ) ∣ n := by
            omega
          contradiction
        · 
          have h₇₃ : Int.ModEq 9 n 3 := by
            simp [Int.ModEq, h₇₂]
          contradiction
        · 
          have h₇₃ : Int.ModEq 9 n 6 := by
            simp [Int.ModEq, h₇₂]
          contradiction
      contradiction
    exact ⟨h₄, Or.inl h₆⟩

have h_subset₂ : ({ n : ℤ | 0 ≤ n ∧ (¬ (3 ∣ n) ∨ 9 ∣ n) } : Set ℤ) ⊆ ({n : ℤ | n ≥ 0 ∧ ¬Int.ModEq 9 n 3 ∧ ¬Int.ModEq 9 n 6} : Set ℤ) := by
  intro n hn
  have h₁ : 0 ≤ n := by simpa using hn.1
  have h₂ : ¬ (3 ∣ n) ∨ 9 ∣ n := by simpa using hn.2
  have h₃ : n ≥ 0 := by linarith

  have h₄ : ¬Int.ModEq 9 n 3 := by
    intro h₄
    have h₅ : (n : ℤ) % 9 = 3 % 9 := by simpa [Int.ModEq] using h₄
    have h₆ : (n : ℤ) % 9 = 3 := by omega
    have h₇ : (3 : ℤ) ∣ n := by
      have h₇₁ : (n : ℤ) % 9 = 3 := h₆
      have h₇₂ : (3 : ℤ) ∣ n := by
        omega
      exact h₇₂
    have h₈ : ¬ (3 ∣ n) ∨ 9 ∣ n := h₂
    cases h₈ with
    | inl h₈ =>
      exfalso
      exact h₈ h₇
    | inr h₈ =>
      have h₉ : (9 : ℤ) ∣ n := h₈
      have h₁₀ : (n : ℤ) % 9 = 0 := by omega
      omega
  have h₅ : ¬Int.ModEq 9 n 6 := by
    intro h₅
    have h₆ : (n : ℤ) % 9 = 6 % 9 := by simpa [Int.ModEq] using h₅
    have h₇ : (n : ℤ) % 9 = 6 := by omega
    have h₈ : (3 : ℤ) ∣ n := by
      have h₈₁ : (n : ℤ) % 9 = 6 := h₇
      have h₈₂ : (3 : ℤ) ∣ n := by
        omega
      exact h₈₂
    have h₉ : ¬ (3 ∣ n) ∨ 9 ∣ n := h₂
    cases h₉ with
    | inl h₉ =>
      exfalso
      exact h₉ h₈
    | inr h₉ =>
      have h₁₀ : (9 : ℤ) ∣ n := h₉
      have h₁₁ : (n : ℤ) % 9 = 0 := by omega
      omega
  exact ⟨h₃, h₄, h₅⟩

have h_main : ({n : ℤ | n ≥ 0 ∧ ¬Int.ModEq 9 n 3 ∧ ¬Int.ModEq 9 n 6} : Set ℤ) = ({ n : ℤ | 0 ≤ n ∧ (¬ (3 ∣ n) ∨ 9 ∣ n) } : Set ℤ) := by
  apply Set.Subset.antisymm
  · 
    exact h_subset₁
  · 
    exact h_subset₂

exact h_main
